text:
In this research, we introduce BEVDepth, a high-performance 3D object detector designed for camera-based Bird’s-Eye-View (BEV) perception. Our approach is motivated by a critical observation: despite the fundamental importance of spatial localization in camera-only 3D detection, the depth estimation quality in existing state-of-the-art models is surprisingly poor. BEVDepth addresses this deficiency by incorporating explicit depth supervision derived from point cloud data. To further boost performance, we introduce a camera-aware depth estimation module that sensitizes the network to varying camera intrinsics and extrinsics. We also design a novel Depth Refinement Module to mitigate the linguistic and spatial errors introduced during the unprojection of image features into 3D space. 

By integrating these innovations with a high-speed Efficient Voxel Pooling strategy and a multi-frame fusion mechanism, BEVDepth achieves a new state-of-the-art performance of 60.9% NDS on the competitive nuScenes test benchmark. This marks the first time a camera-only model has surpassed the 60% NDS threshold while maintaining sufficient computational efficiency for real-world applications. Our code has been made publicly available.
